testBaseline.note: Baseline from Sprint 10 close (8/8 stories, B-037 crawl phase COMPLETE, merged to main@9d7fa98). 1977 Windows fast-suite passed. Pi aarch64 baseline from US-182: 1583 passed + 47 pi_only in tests/pi/hardware/ on chi-eclipse-01 Python 3.13.5.

sprintNotes: This is the Walk phase of B-037. Goal: simulator data flows Pi -> Chi-Srv-01 via real HTTP, server CLI reports reflect it, display shows sync status. Sprint exit is a manual end-to-end validation (US-166).

AGENT MODEL: Hybrid (same as Sprint 10). Ralph writes code on Windows + SSHes to mcornelison@10.27.27.28 for Pi deployment/validation. Key-based SSH is working. Server is already live at chi-srv-01:8000 from Sprint 9 close (9 drives, 26265 realtime rows, 18 connection events). Do NOT modify src/server/ this sprint -- it's working; Pi-side is the subject.

PI STATE ON SPRINT START: Sprint 10 shipped 8/8. hostname=chi-eclipse-01. systemd service installed but BL-004 means systemctl start fails without --simulate -- US-185 fixes that early so the rest of the sprint can use systemd-native boot testing. HardwareManager boots at runtime (TD-014 closed). UpsMonitor reads real MAX17048 chip for VCELL/SOC/CRATE; getPowerSource() is broken (I-015) -- US-184 fixes that.

SERVER STATE ON SPRINT START: chi-srv-01:8000 healthy. Endpoints exist from B-036: POST /api/v1/sync (delta upsert), POST /api/v1/backup (binary file receive), GET /api/v1/ping (auth probe), POST /api/v1/analyze (on-demand AI). Authentication: X-API-Key header sourced from COMPANION_API_KEY env var server-side. The sync endpoint accepts JSON with source_device + source_id -- matches ID-mapping decision in specs/architecture.md.

SPRINT-BRANCH WORKFLOW (Rule 8): runs on sprint/pi-walk (created from main by PM). Marcus committed this sprint setup. Ralph leaves all code unstaged; Marcus commits at sprint close + merges to main. See feedback_ralph_no_git_commands.md.

TWO HIGH-RISK DATA-INTEGRITY ITEMS this sprint: (1) US-149 SyncClient must NEVER advance the sync_log high-water mark on a failed push -- data loss on the Pi side is unrecoverable without a restore. Write the failure-path test FIRST. (2) US-184 rewrites getPowerSource() semantics -- if wrong, graceful-shutdown paths fail silently. Include the CIO physical unplug drill in the AC, and keep readExt5vVoltageFromVcgencmd() as a diagnostic field even after the signal is no longer used for source detection.

NO CAR THIS SPRINT. Walk phase is bench-only. Bluetooth pairing + real OBD-II + VIN decode = Run phase (Sprint 12+). This sprint proves the sync pipeline + corrects the two Sprint 10 rough edges (BL-004 systemd + I-015 power source).

STOP EARLY IF SSH BREAKS. Any story depending on Pi SSH must verify connectivity first (ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'hostname') and STOP with a clear message if that fails. Don't power through SSH errors.

DECISION CAPTURED 2026-04-18 (PM): BL-004 resolution = Option A (edit service file), NOT Option B (flip config default). Rationale: --simulate flag in the unit file is the cleaner separation -- config.json stays honest about what simulator mode means, and flipping it to default true would be trivially easy to forget when the ECU is wired. US-185 delivers the edit.

DECISION CAPTURED 2026-04-18 (PM): I-015 resolution = US-184 in-sprint fix, NOT carried forward. Rationale: shipping a broken getPowerSource() that always returns EXTERNAL would mask real UPS-power-loss events -- if the Pi's in the car and wall power dies, the shutdown path needs to fire. Fix it before Walk phase closes so Run phase can depend on it.

EXECUTION ORDER (suggested): US-185 first (S, unblocks systemd), US-184 second (M, fixes the outstanding hardware bug), then the sync vertical slice US-148 -> US-151 -> US-149 -> US-154 -> US-166 (last is the sprint-exit validation). Only US-166 strictly requires the server to be reachable; everything else can progress on Windows if SSH breaks.

stories.title: UpsMonitor power-source detection via VCELL trend (fix I-015)
stories.intent: Replace the broken getPowerSource() implementation. The existing vcgencmd pmic_read_adc EXT5V_V signal never distinguishes wall power from UPS-boost-from-LiPo because the X1209 HAT regulates the rail to ~5V in both modes (that's what a UPS does). Use a VCELL-trend heuristic over a rolling window. When CIO physically unplugs wall power, VCELL drops; that's the real signal. Prefer CRATE polarity when the chip variant exposes it.

filesToTouch: config.json (new pi.hardware.upsMonitor section -- window, slope threshold)
specs/architecture.md (power-source detection note -- EXT5V was wrong, VCELL trend + CRATE preferred)

groundingRefs.unit: VCELL live readings from CIO's Session 20 drill
CRATE under UPS discharge (Session 20); 0xFFFF on Session 44 live chip (disabled variant)

groundingRefs.source: PM judgment -- long enough to smooth noisy slope, short enough to respond to a physical unplug within sprint attention span

groundingRefs.unit: default VCELL slope threshold V/min to declare BATTERY (configurable)
groundingRefs.source: Session 20 saw 4.181 -> 3.66 over ~10 min bench drill, slope ~ -0.05 V/min; -0.02 is a safety margin
groundingRefs.owner: Spool (tunable via Spool review if edge cases surface in Run phase)

acceptance: UpsMonitor maintains a rolling buffer of (timestampMonotonic, vcellVolts, socPercent) -- one entry per polling iteration. Buffer cap = ceil(historyWindowSeconds / pollIntervalSeconds) + 2
config.json pi.hardware.upsMonitor adds: historyWindowSeconds (default 60), vcellSlopeThresholdVoltsPerMinute (default -0.02), crateThresholdPercentPerHour (default -0.05)

getPowerSource() decision: if CRATE is not None AND CRATE < crateThresholdPercentPerHour -> BATTERY. Else if VCELL slope over the window < vcellSlopeThresholdVoltsPerMinute -> BATTERY. Else -> EXTERNAL. If insufficient history (< 2 samples) AND CRATE is None -> return current cached source (start as EXTERNAL on first boot).
readExt5vVoltageFromVcgencmd() RETAINED as a diagnostic read exposed via telemetry, but NOT called from getPowerSource()

CIO physical unplug drill passes: 5 baseline readings show source=EXTERNAL with wall power; CIO unplugs USB-C from wall; within historyWindowSeconds (default 60s), source flips to BATTERY; CIO re-plugs; within historyWindowSeconds, source recovers to EXTERNAL. Ralph produces a one-page checklist for the CIO; Ralph does NOT execute the physical unplug himself.
onPowerSourceChange callback fires exactly once per transition (no flapping at the slope-crossing boundary -- 1-sample hysteresis is OK)
Graceful degradation: if VCELL read raises AND CRATE is None, return PowerSource.UNKNOWN and log a warning once per state-change (not every poll)

PowerSource enum: add UNKNOWN if not already present; keep external/battery values unchanged. Update consumers (shutdown_handler, telemetry_logger, hardware_manager getStatus) to handle UNKNOWN gracefully (treat as 'don't trigger shutdown' default)
Update specs/architecture.md power-source detection paragraph -- remove 'EXT5V via vcgencmd' framing, add VCELL-trend + CRATE-preferred framing with one-sentence note on HAT regulation
Close offices/pm/issues/I-015 with a Resolution section pointing to this commit + evidence
Completion note includes: drill evidence (5 baseline + N battery + N replug readings with timestamps), slope math sample, CRATE values observed on the live chip, test counts Windows + Pi, ruff clean
verification: pytest tests/pi/hardware/ -v on Windows (all pre-existing tests still green) + Pi
Live CIO unplug drill on chi-eclipse-01 (Ralph produces the checklist, CIO runs it, Ralph captures the journal output)
invariants: Do NOT remove readExt5vVoltageFromVcgencmd() -- keep as diagnostic telemetry
Do NOT call sudo from Python
Never block on I2C or subprocess -- always timeout
Do NOT change the PowerSource enum shape beyond adding UNKNOWN without PM discussion
Do NOT lose the 32 Sprint 10 tests -- update fixtures but keep the semantics they verify
stopConditions: STOP if VCELL does not drop within historyWindowSeconds of physical unplug during the CIO drill -- either the HAT isn't switching to battery (power-path issue) or polling is too slow. Surface findings to PM; do NOT silently widen thresholds to compensate
STOP if CRATE still reads 0xFFFF AND VCELL-trend doesn't give a clean signal -- measurement variance or Geekworm-specific register may need investigation
STOP if existing Sprint 10 tests regress -- update fixtures to match new semantics rather than deleting coverage

stories.title: Pi sync_log table + delta query helper
stories.intent: Add a sync_log table to the Pi SQLite that tracks per-table high-water marks (last_synced_id) for delta sync. Add a getDeltaRows helper that returns rows with id greater than the high-water mark, ascending. No sync pushes yet -- just the storage layer.

filesToTouch: specs/architecture.md (document sync_log table in the Pi schema section)

groundingRefs.value: realtime_data, statistics, profiles, vehicle_info, ai_recommendations, connection_log, alert_log, calibration_sessions

acceptance: sync_log table DDL: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sync_log (table_name TEXT PRIMARY KEY, last_synced_id INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, last_synced_at TEXT, last_batch_id TEXT, status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('ok','pending','failed')))
Schema init idempotent: running initDb() twice leaves the table unchanged (no duplicate errors)

getDeltaRows(conn, tableName, lastId, limit) returns a list of rows (dicts) with id > lastId, ORDER BY id ASC, LIMIT limit. lastId=0 returns from the start. tableName must be in the in-scope list -- reject unknown names with ValueError (SQL injection guard)

updateHighWaterMark(conn, tableName, lastId, batchId, status='ok'): UPSERT -- insert if missing, update if present. Advances last_synced_id + last_synced_at + last_batch_id + status atomically (single transaction)
getHighWaterMark(conn, tableName) returns (last_synced_id, last_synced_at, last_batch_id, status) or (0, None, None, 'pending') if no row yet
Integration test: fresh DB -> initDb -> insert 5 rows into realtime_data -> getDeltaRows returns 5 -> updateHighWaterMark to id=5 -> insert 3 more -> getDeltaRows returns 3 -> full cycle again
Reject-unknown-table test: getDeltaRows(conn, 'malicious; DROP TABLE x;--', 0, 10) raises ValueError
Completion note includes: schema DDL, test counts, sample query output
invariants: Do NOT touch server-side models -- the server already has its sync infrastructure (B-036)
Do NOT write a push-to-server path yet -- US-149 owns that
Do NOT add sync_log rows for Pi-only tables (battery_log, power_log)
ID columns are INTEGER -- never treat as strings
stopConditions: STOP if schema init breaks existing Pi tests -- integration tests may depend on the current schema; surface to PM rather than silently fixing
STOP if the production Pi's SQLite has rows that would conflict (vanishingly unlikely for a new table, but check)

stories.intent: Add a companionService section to config.json under pi: that describes how to reach Chi-Srv-01. Add COMPANION_API_KEY to .env.example. Teach ConfigValidator the defaults. No network calls yet -- just the config surface.

acceptance: config.json under pi: gains companionService: {enabled: true, baseUrl: 'http://10.27.27.120:8000', apiKeyEnv: 'COMPANION_API_KEY', syncTimeoutSeconds: 30, batchSize: 500, retryMaxAttempts: 3, retryBackoffSeconds: [1,2,4,8,16]}
.env.example adds COMPANION_API_KEY=your-api-key-here with an inline comment
src/common/config/validator.py DEFAULTS gets all pi.companionService.* keys with defaults matching config.json
ConfigValidator accepts the new section without complaint (unit test proves)
ConfigValidator rejects malformed values (negative timeout, batchSize < 1, retryBackoffSeconds non-list) with ConfigurationError
When pi.companionService.enabled is false, downstream consumers return early (this AC is just the config surface; short-circuit lives in US-149)
Completion note includes: config.json diff, .env.example diff, validator test count, ConfigValidator round-trip sample
verification: python validate_config.py succeeds on a fresh config.json
invariants: Do NOT hardcode the API key -- always via ${COMPANION_API_KEY} env placeholder + secrets_loader
Do NOT add keys not in the spec -- keep the surface minimal
Do NOT modify server-side -- server owns its own secret surface
stopConditions: STOP if validate_config.py fails after edits -- surface the schema error
STOP if .env handling would require secrets_loader changes that break existing consumers -- surface to PM

stories.title: Pi HTTP sync client (SyncClient)
stories.intent: Build a SyncClient class that reads the sync_log, fetches delta rows, POSTs them to the server with X-API-Key auth, and advances the high-water mark ON SUCCESS ONLY. Exponential backoff retry. The critical invariant: a failed push must NEVER advance the high-water mark -- that's what protects Pi-side data from loss when the server is unreachable.

filesToTouch: requirements-pi.txt (add requests or httpx if not already present)

groundingRefs.source: server auth -- check src/server/ for the exact header

acceptance: SyncClient(config) constructor reads pi.companionService config + COMPANION_API_KEY env var via secrets_loader. Raises ConfigurationError if apiKey missing

pushDelta(tableName) -> PushResult: reads sync_log high-water mark -> gets delta rows via getDeltaRows -> POSTs to {baseUrl}/api/v1/sync with JSON body {source_device, table, rows} and X-API-Key header -> on 2xx, updateHighWaterMark to max(row.id) + status='ok'. PushResult fields: tableName, rowsPushed, batchId, elapsed, status
pushAllDeltas() iterates the in-scope tables (same list as US-148), returns List[PushResult]

Retry logic: on ConnectionError, Timeout, or 5xx -> retry with backoff per retryBackoffSeconds up to retryMaxAttempts. On 4xx except 429 -> fail immediately (client bug, don't retry). On 429 -> retry with backoff.
CRITICAL: failed push (all retries exhausted) does NOT advance the high-water mark. sync_log row updated with status='failed' + last_batch_id + last_synced_at but last_synced_id unchanged
When config.companionService.enabled is false, pushDelta returns PushResult(status='disabled', rowsPushed=0) immediately -- no network call
Test: mocked-HTTP success path advances high-water mark correctly across 2 push cycles
Test: mocked-HTTP 500 fails all retries and does NOT advance high-water mark (CRITICAL invariant)
Test: mocked-HTTP timeout fails all retries and does NOT advance high-water mark
Test: mocked-HTTP 4xx (401/403) fails immediately (no retry), does NOT advance high-water mark
Test: mocked-HTTP 429 retries with backoff
Test: missing COMPANION_API_KEY raises ConfigurationError at construction
Test: unknown table name raises ValueError (inherited from US-148 guard)
Completion note includes: PushResult sample outputs, test counts, CRITICAL-invariant test output verbatim
invariants: NEVER advance sync_log.last_synced_id on a failed or partial push -- data integrity, no exceptions
NEVER write secrets to logs
NEVER crash the orchestrator if the network is down -- return PushResult(status='failed')
Timeouts must come from config.syncTimeoutSeconds, not hardcoded
Do NOT add auto-scheduling/background sync -- US-154 + later stories own scheduling
stopConditions: STOP if the server /api/v1/sync contract differs from what the spec implies -- surface the divergence to PM rather than guessing the payload shape
STOP if src/common/errors/handler.py already has a retry helper Ralph should reuse -- reuse beats duplication
STOP if requests/httpx addition causes unrelated test failures -- surface to PM

stories.title: Manual sync CLI (scripts/sync_now.py)
stories.intent: Add a CLI that invokes SyncClient.pushAllDeltas() and prints human-readable output. This is the Walk-phase trigger -- the CIO runs it manually to sync Pi to Chi-Srv-01. Auto-sync on WiFi return is Sprint-phase scope.
acceptance: scripts/sync_now.py runs via 'python scripts/sync_now.py' (no args for baseline case)
Loads config via existing pipeline (validate_config pattern), constructs SyncClient
Prints a start line with timestamp + config summary (baseUrl, batchSize)
For each in-scope table, prints '<table>: N new rows -> pushed -> accepted (batch: <id>)' on success or '<table>: failed -> <reason>' on failure
Prints total rows pushed + elapsed seconds + overall status (OK / FAILED / DISABLED)
Exit 0 if all pushes succeeded (or all short-circuited via disabled config). Exit 1 if any push failed
'Nothing to sync' handling: empty DB or no delta rows exits 0 with a clear message
Unreachable-server handling: prints error, exits 1, sync_log rows reflect failed status (US-149 invariant -- no high-water advance)
--help prints usage. --dry-run queries delta counts but doesn't push (optional, cheap)
Test: mocked SyncClient returning all OK -> exits 0, output matches format
Test: mocked SyncClient returning one failure -> exits 1, output shows the failure
Test: 'Nothing to sync' -> exits 0 with empty-summary message
Completion note includes: sample CLI output from a Pi run (if reachable) or mocked SyncClient on Windows
invariants: Do NOT add scheduling (cron, systemd timer, background thread) -- manual-only in Walk phase
Do NOT surface secrets in output -- mask API key in any config-dump lines
Do NOT require the server to be reachable -- CLI must run offline and produce a clean 'failed' path
stopConditions: STOP if SyncClient construction fails at import time -- suggests tight runtime coupling that needs PM redesign
STOP if CLI output is ambiguous on mixed success/failure -- that IS the interesting case; design explicitly

stories.title: End-to-end simulator-to-server validation
stories.intent: Sprint exit: prove the Walk-phase pipeline end to end. Simulator runs on chi-eclipse-01 -> writes to local SQLite -> sync_now.py pushes to Chi-Srv-01 -> MariaDB row counts match -> server CLI report renders correctly. This is the sprint's exit criterion.

filesToTouch: tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_e2e.py (new -- CI-friendly with mocked server OR skipped by default)
scripts/validate_pi_to_server.sh (new -- bash driver the CIO runs, mirrors the manual checklist)

acceptance: scripts/validate_pi_to_server.sh is a CIO-runnable driver executing the 7-step validation from spec 2.5: (1) run simulator full_cycle on Pi, (2) verify local SQLite row counts, (3) run sync_now.py, (4) verify MariaDB row counts on Chi-Srv-01, (5) run scripts/report.py --drive latest on server, (6) confirm output matches expectations (non-empty, correct drive, statistics present), (7) verify display Sync indicator flipped to green (manual visual, optional if display not connected)
The driver prints a PASS/FAIL line after each step and an overall PASS/FAIL at the end

tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_e2e.py: deterministic mocked-server version (httpx_mock or a local test-server fixture) -- proves Pi-side plumbing without requiring Chi-Srv-01 to be up. Runs in CI normally.
Completion note includes: full script output from a live CIO run on chi-eclipse-01 + chi-srv-01, MariaDB row count deltas (before/after), report.py output excerpt
If any step fails, the script surfaces the exact failure and exits non-zero. CIO should be able to identify the broken layer from the output alone
Update offices/pm/backlog/B-037.md adding a 'Walk Phase Exit Evidence' section with the run output
Row counts match exactly across layers: if simulator wrote 1000 realtime_data + 5 connection_log rows, MariaDB must show +1000 and +5. Mismatch = FAIL.
Sprint-close invariant: this is the LAST story in Sprint 11. Do not begin until US-154 is passed
verification: pytest tests/integration/test_pi_to_server_e2e.py -v (mocked version, must pass in CI)
bash scripts/validate_pi_to_server.sh (live version, CIO runs manually on both machines)
invariants: Do NOT modify server code -- server is out of scope. If a discrepancy surfaces server-side, file an issue; don't patch
Do NOT require the Pi to be in the car -- Walk is bench-only
Do NOT use any real OBD-II data -- simulator only
stopConditions: STOP if MariaDB row counts don't match after a clean sync -- that's a real bug somewhere in the stack, not something to paper over
STOP if report.py --drive latest produces stale or empty output -- means the sync didn't actually land, or server analytics haven't picked it up
STOP if the CIO's live run fails after the CI mocked version passes -- the mock is hiding something real
